title: alsine weberbaueri
intro: Alsine weberbaueri, also known as the Andean creeping saxifrage, is a species of flowering plant in the saxifrage family, Saxifragaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its medicinal properties include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alsine weberbaueri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and soothe respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used to make a topical ointment or salve, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
